claim: High-sensitivity cardiac troponin T (HSCT-T) dosage may not be diagnostic if the onset of symptoms occurs less than 3 hours before acute myocardial injury (AMI).

result.claim: High-sensitivity cardiac troponin T (HSCT-T) dosage may not be diagnostic if the onset of symptoms occurs less than 3 hours before acute myocardial injury (AMI).
result.reasoning: The claim is supported by evidence from a systematic review and meta-analysis (Paper ID: 34873974). Specifically, Chunk ID: chunk_459 states that a single baseline measurement of the Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay "may not be appropriate for patients who present less than three hours after symptom onset" for ruling out acute myocardial infarction. While the paper is a systematic review and meta-analysis, its credibility is somewhat limited by the unknown publication year and zero citations. However, the statement directly addresses and supports the claim.

evidence.text: The Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay can be used to rule out acute myocardial infarction.
evidence.source: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis

evidence.text: The systematic review and meta-analysis analyzed the accuracy of a single baseline measurement of the Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction.
evidence.source: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis

evidence.text: Plasma L-carnitine levels in 2,595 subjects undergoing cardiac evaluation predicted increased risks for prevalent cardiovascular disease and incident major adverse cardiac events.
evidence.source: Intestinal microbiota metabolism of L-carnitine, a nutrient in red meat, promotes atherosclerosis

evidence.text: The hazard ratio using maximum follow-up time was 1.4 (95% CI, 1.1-1.8) for individuals with 1 high biomarker.
evidence.source: Inflammatory biomarkers and exacerbations in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

evidence.text: 46 patients without acute myocardial infarction will test positive if the 3-5 ng/L cut-off value is used.
evidence.source: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis

evidence.text: The hazard ratio using maximum follow-up time was 1.6 (95% CI, 1.3-2.2) for individuals with 2 high biomarkers.
evidence.source: Inflammatory biomarkers and exacerbations in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

evidence.text: The study included 3315 case patients with a diagnosis of first-time acute myocardial infarction between 1992 and 1997.
evidence.source: Antibiotics and risk of subsequent first-time acute myocardial infarction.

evidence.text: The hazard ratio using maximum follow-up time was 2.5 (95% CI, 1.8-3.4) for individuals with 3 high biomarkers.
evidence.source: Inflammatory biomarkers and exacerbations in chronic obstructive pulmonary disease.

evidence.text: Serious cardiovascular events included myocardial infarction (MI), sudden cardiac death (SCD), or stroke.
evidence.source: ADHD medications and risk of serious cardiovascular events in young and middle-aged adults.

evidence.text: Studies evaluating the diagnostic accuracy of a single baseline measurement of the Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for the diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction were included.
evidence.source: Diagnostic accuracy of single baseline measurement of Elecsys Troponin T high-sensitive assay for diagnosis of acute myocardial infarction in emergency department: systematic review and meta-analysis
